title: Inversie · V2 word order
subtitle: Subject jumps over the verb when anything else leads

questions.explanation: When a time word like 'Vandaag' takes slot 1, the verb MUST be in slot 2 and the subject moves to slot 3: Vandaag [1] · werk [2] · ik [3] · thuis. This is the V2 rule.

questions.q: Fix this sentence: 'Om acht uur de trein vertrekt.'
options: Om acht uur de trein vertrekt.
De trein om acht uur vertrekt.
Om acht uur vertrekt de trein.
Vertrekt om acht uur de trein.
questions.explanation: 'Om acht uur' fills slot 1. The conjugated verb 'vertrekt' MUST be in slot 2. The subject 'de trein' goes to slot 3. Result: 'Om acht uur vertrekt de trein.'

questions.q: Which jij/je form is correct in a question?
questions.explanation: When 'jij' or 'je' comes AFTER the verb, the -t is dropped. 'Jij werkt' (correct) but 'Werk jij?' (correct — no -t in inversion). Same rule: 'Vandaag werk je thuis.'

questions.q: 'Je kunt oefeningen maken op onze website.' → Front 'Op onze website'. What is the correct inversion?
options: Op onze website je kunt oefeningen maken.
Op onze website kunt je oefeningen maken.
Op onze website kun je oefeningen maken.
Op onze website kun jij oefeningen maken kun.
questions.explanation: Special form: 'kunt' becomes 'kun' when 'je' follows. So: 'Op onze website kun je oefeningen maken.' Same with 'wilt' → 'wil je'.

options: Mijn buurman kijkt naar het journaal.
questions.explanation: ''s Morgens' is a fronted time → triggers inversion. Subject 'ik' moves after the verb 'drink'. The other sentences all have the subject in slot 1, so they're default (non-inverted) order.

questions.q: Separable verb 'opstaan' — which inverted sentence is correct?
options: Om zeven uur staat Stefaan op.
Om zeven uur Stefaan staat op.
questions.explanation: Even under inversion, separable verbs SPLIT — 'staat' fills the V2 slot, 'op' stays at the END. The order is: Time [1] · main verb [2] · subject · ... · prefix [end].

questions.q: 'Hij wil thuis werken.' → Front 'Vandaag'. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: The modal 'wil' moves to slot 2 (V2 rule). The infinitive 'werken' STAYS at the end. Subject 'hij' slots after the modal: 'Vandaag wil hij thuis werken.'

questions.q: When is the SUBJECT in position 1?
options: When the sentence starts with the subject (default order)
Whenever the verb has no prefix
questions.explanation: Default Dutch order is Subject + Verb + everything else, just like English. Inversion only kicks in when SOMETHING ELSE (time, place, question word, object) takes slot 1, pushing the subject to slot 3.

questions.q: Why does 'In Brussel mijn zus woont' sound wrong to a Dutch speaker?
options: The verb must be in slot 2, but here 'woont' is in slot 4
Dutch doesn't allow place words to start a sentence
questions.explanation: V2 rule violation. 'In Brussel' fills slot 1, so the verb 'woont' MUST be in slot 2: 'In Brussel woont mijn zus.' Dutch never lets the verb drift past position 2 in a main clause.

questions.q: Which time expression does NOT trigger inversion when used at the start?
questions.explanation: ANY non-subject element at slot 1 triggers inversion. Time words (vandaag, 's morgens, om acht uur, in het weekend, op maandag, elke avond, soms…) all behave the same way: subject moves after the verb.
